title: Blender Live Sync Cinema 4D
description:
Blender Live Sync for Cinema 4D — Addon Overview

Blender Live Sync for Cinema 4D is a real-time synchronization plugin designed to streamline workflows between Cinema 4D (C4D) and Blender. It enables near-zero-latency updates, allowing artists to model, deform, sculpt, rig, and animate in C4D while seeing the results instantly in Blender—eliminating repetitive exporting and importing.

🚀 Real-Time Synchronization

Work in Cinema 4D and watch your changes update in Blender instantly. Deform, sculpt, and bind bones to your models in C4D, with real-time updates reflected in Blender for faster iteration and smoother production.


✅ Broad Feature Support

The plugin supports a wide range of data types and objects commonly used in production pipelines:


Model Data

Real-time transfer of essential mesh information, including:

  • Vertices and topology

  • UVs, normals, and more

Deformers

Supports all deformable devices that affect geometric deformation, including:

  • Deformers, fields/domains, and related systems

Generators

Geometry generated by C4D generators is updated in real time, making procedural workflows fully sync-ready.

Motion Graphics (MoGraph)

Synchronizes motion graphics objects such as:

  • Cloners

  • Matrices

  • Other MoGraph elements

Bones / Rigging

Real-time synchronization of skeletal deformation, enabling efficient rigging and animation workflows.

Sculpting / Carving

Models modified by sculpting (carving brushes) update instantly in Blender, keeping sculpt workflows fast and responsive.

Camera Sync

Synchronizes camera parameters including:

  • Position and rotation

  • Focal length and other camera settings

Timeline Sync




Keeps both applications aligned with:

  • Current frame

  • Start and end frames

  • Frame rate synchronization

⚡ High Performance




Powered by shared memory technology, the plugin transfers only the data that changes—achieving extremely fast performance with almost zero latency.

🧭 Configurable Axis Mapping

Customize coordinate system conversion from C4D to Blender, with support for:

  • Axis mapping adjustments

  • Scaling

  • Offset controls
documentation:
Blender Live Sync for Cinema 4D


Installation & Setup Guide

Blender Live Sync for Cinema 4D is a real-time synchronization plugin that connects Cinema 4D (C4D) and Blender, enabling near-zero-latency updates for meshes, deformers, generators, MoGraph objects, bones, sculpting changes, cameras, and timeline settings.

This document explains how to install the plugin on both sides and how to establish the first successful connection.


Table of Contents

  1. System Requirements

  2. Package Contents

  3. Installing the Cinema 4D Plugin

  4. Installing the Blender Add-on

  5. First-Time Connection Setup

  6. Verifying the Sync

  7. Axis Mapping Setup

  8. Troubleshooting

  9. Support

1. System Requirements

Supported Software

  • Cinema 4D: R25 / 2023 / 2024 or newer (recommended)

  • Blender: 3.x / 4.x (recommended)

Operating Systems

  • Windows / macOS (depending on your plugin build)

Recommended Setup

For best performance and lowest latency, run Cinema 4D and Blender on the same computer.
(Some versions may support network syncing, depending on your release.)

2. Package Contents

Your download package typically contains:

  • Cinema 4D Plugin Folder

    • Example: BlenderLiveSync_C4D/

  • Blender Add-on

    • Example: BlenderLiveSync_Blender.zip or BlenderLiveSync.py

  • Optional documentation files:

    • README.txt, Manual.pdf, etc.

⚠️ Note: Folder names may differ depending on the plugin version.

3. Installing the Cinema 4D Plugin (C4D Side)

Option A (Recommended): Install into the C4D plugins Folder

  1. Close Cinema 4D (if running).

  2. Locate your Cinema 4D installation directory:

Windows (example):
C:\Program Files\Maxon Cinema 4D 2024\plugins\

macOS (example):
Applications/Maxon Cinema 4D 2024/plugins/

  1. Copy the plugin folder into the plugins directory.

Example:
BlenderLiveSync_C4D/ → .../Cinema 4D/plugins/

  1. Launch Cinema 4D.

  2. Verify the plugin appears in one of these locations:

  • Extensions menu

  • Plugins menu

  • A dedicated plugin panel / toolbar

Option B: Install into the User Plugins Folder (No Admin Permission Needed)

If you cannot access the system installation directory:

  1. Open Cinema 4D

  2. Go to: Edit → Preferences

  3. Locate: Plugins

  4. Find the User Plugins Path

  5. Copy the plugin folder into that directory

  6. Restart Cinema 4D

4. Installing the Blender Add-on (Blender Side)

  1. Open Blender

  2. Go to: Edit → Preferences → Add-ons

  3. Click Install…

  4. Select the add-on file:

    • If you received a .zip, install the .zip directly (do not unzip)

    • If you received a .py, select the .py file

  5. After installation, search for the add-on name:
    Blender Live Sync for Cinema 4D

  6. Enable the add-on by checking the box

After enabling, the plugin UI usually appears in:

  • 3D Viewport → Sidebar (press N)

  • or an add-on tab in the Blender interface (depending on version)

5. First-Time Connection Setup

Once both plugins are installed, follow these steps to connect.

Step 1: Start Sync in Cinema 4D

  1. Open your C4D scene

  2. Open the plugin panel from Extensions / Plugins

  3. Click one of the following (name may vary):

  • Start

  • Connect

  • Enable Sync

  • Live Sync On

  1. Confirm the plugin status shows something similar to:

  • Running

  • Waiting for Blender

  • Connected

Step 2: Start Sync in Blender

  1. Open Blender

  2. Open the plugin panel (usually in the N-panel)

  3. Click:

  • Connect

  • Start Sync

  • Receive

  1. If the plugin supports connection settings, confirm:

  • Connection mode = Shared Memory

  • Status = Connected

6. Verifying the Sync

After connecting, use the following tests to confirm everything works correctly.

6.1 Mesh Update Test

  1. In Cinema 4D, create a cube (or any primitive)

  2. Move, rotate, or scale it

  3. The object should update in Blender immediately

6.2 Topology / UV / Normal Test

  1. In C4D, convert a primitive to editable mesh

  2. Modify points / edges / polygons

  3. Confirm Blender updates the mesh shape correctly

6.3 Deformer Test

  1. Add a deformer in C4D (Bend / Twist / etc.)

  2. Adjust deformer strength and settings

  3. Blender should update in real time

6.4 Generator & MoGraph Test

  1. Create a generator or MoGraph object in C4D

  2. Change its parameters (clone count, spacing, etc.)

  3. Blender should update the generated geometry

6.5 Bone Deformation Test

  1. Bind bones to a mesh in C4D

  2. Move/rotate bones

  3. Blender should update deformation instantly

6.6 Camera Sync Test

  1. Select a camera in C4D

  2. Adjust:

    • Position / rotation

    • Focal length

  3. Blender camera should match the changes

6.7 Timeline Sync Test

  1. In C4D, scrub the timeline or press play

  2. Confirm Blender synchronizes:

    • Current frame

    • Start frame / end frame

    • Frame rate (FPS)

7. Axis Mapping Setup (Coordinate Conversion)

Cinema 4D and Blender use different coordinate systems.
If you notice incorrect orientation (flipped axes, wrong rotation, scale mismatch), configure the axis mapping:

  1. Open plugin settings (C4D side or Blender side depending on version)

  2. Find Axis Mapping / Coordinate Conversion

  3. Adjust:

  • Axis conversion (X/Y/Z mapping)

  • Scale

  • Offset

Tip: If your model appears rotated 90° or mirrored, axis mapping is usually the cause.

8. Troubleshooting

Issue: Plugin does not appear in Cinema 4D

Solutions:

  • Confirm the plugin folder is placed in the correct plugins directory

  • Make sure the folder is not nested incorrectly
    (Example: avoid plugins/BlenderLiveSync/BlenderLiveSync/)

  • Restart Cinema 4D

  • Try installing via the User Plugins Path

Issue: Add-on does not appear in Blender

Solutions:

  • Make sure it is enabled in Preferences → Add-ons

  • Use the search bar and type Live Sync

  • Check the N-panel in the 3D Viewport

Issue: No connection / no updates

Solutions:

  • Ensure both plugins are running

  • Restart both Cinema 4D and Blender

  • Try re-clicking Connect / Start Sync

  • Confirm the plugin is using Shared Memory mode (if available)

Issue: Lag or slow performance

Solutions:

  • Reduce the number of synced objects

  • Avoid syncing extremely high-poly sculpt meshes during heavy edits

  • Use shared memory mode for best speed

  • Sync only changing data (recommended workflow)

Issue: Wrong scale or rotation in Blender

Solutions:

  • Configure Axis Mapping

  • Apply scale/offset settings

  • Verify both scenes use consistent units

9. Support

If you still have issues, please prepare the following information before contacting support:

  • Cinema 4D version

  • Blender version

  • Operating system

  • Plugin version

  • A short description of the issue

  • Screenshots of plugin status panels (C4D + Blender)
